pi_items.source: task.json task description requires these three modules; setup_task.sh creates the initial monolith to be refactored into these modules
setup_task.sh creates Pet.java, Owner.java in package com.petclinic.model (lines 40-61) and DateFormatter.java in package com.petclinic.util (lines 64-73)
setup_task.sh creates ClinicService.java and ClinicServiceImpl.java in package com.petclinic.service (lines 76-101)
setup_task.sh creates PetClinicApp.java in package com.petclinic.app (lines 104-121)

privileged_info_summary: The split_into_modules task operates on a synthetic Maven project (petclinic-mono) created at /home/ga/IdeaProjects/petclinic-mono. The monolith contains four Java packages distributed across: petclinic-model module (receiving com/petclinic/model and com/petclinic/util packages), petclinic-service module (receiving com/petclinic/service package), and petclinic-app module (receiving com/petclinic/app package). The project uses Maven 4.0.0 with Java 17 compilation target. The setup script creates concrete Java classes: Pet.java and Owner.java in the model package, DateFormatter.java utility class, ClinicService.java interface and ClinicServiceImpl.java implementation in the service package, and PetClinicApp.java main entry point. The task requires refactoring this initial single-module structure into a proper multi-module Maven project with a parent POM and three child modules with appropriate inter-module dependencies (service depends on model, app depends on both service and model).
